seasonArc: Noemi Rüegg's 2026 spring marked her arrival as a genuine Monument contender. The Swiss rider was at her sharpest at Milano-Sanremo Donne, following the decisive Poggio acceleration into the winning quintet and finishing runner-up on the Via Roma, glued to Lotte Kopecky's wheel and clear of the chasing peloton. It was the standout result of her early season — a near-miss at a Monument that beat all but one of the sport's best finishers. The cobbled classics were harder going, a 12th at the Ronde van Vlaanderen (+1:58) showing the gap between her best one-day form and the attritional terrain, but her Sanremo second was the clearest signal yet of a rider stepping up to the front rank of the classics.
